metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_2923
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.3: "USP46 deubiquitinates glutamate receptor subunit 1 (GluA1) in vivo and in vitro"

Fig.5: "Knockdown of USP46 reduces both surface and total AMPARs in hippocampal neurons"

Fig.6: "Over-expression of USP46 increases surface AMPAR expression"

Fig.7: "Effect of USP46 on AMPAR internalization"

Fig.8: "USP46 regulates AMPAR mediated synaptic currents"

Notes:
- The authors remark that "Ubiquitinated surface AMPARs are marked for efficient endocytosis, leading to inhibited synaptic transmission (Lin et al. 2011)."
- In Fig.6 the authors show "We found that the surface GluA1 was significantly enhanced by USP46 over-expression. Quantification indicated a significant difference between EGFP control and cells with USP46 over-expression (Fig. 6a and b) (EGFP: 1 ± 0.05, n = 9 cells; USP46: 1.20 ± 0.05, n = 10 cells, p = 0.002). In contrast, no significant changes were detected in total GluA1 in neurons expressing either USP46 or USP12 (Fig. 6c and d) (EGFP: 1.0 ± 0.07, n = 9 cells; USP46: 1.08 ± 0.09, n = 9 cells, p = 0.20)." This argues for a role of USP46 in regulation of AMPAR endocytosis and not in protein degradation. I have refrained from making the annotation to term 'regulation of protein catabolic process at postsynapse, modulating synaptic transmission (GO:0099576)' because not sufficiently supported by the data.. Experimental description: - "Primary cultured cortical and hippocampal neurons were prepared from embryonic day 18 rat embryos as previously described (Hou et al. 2008)."
- "Cortical tissue dissected from adult rat brains was ...."
- Over-expressed USP46 was of rat species: "Full length rat USP46 and USP12 were PCR amplified t..."
